steps.detail: Bring a pot of salted water to a gentle simmer and rain in the polenta in a slow stream, whisking so it doesn't clump. Stir often for about 30 min (or the packet time for quick polenta) until thick, smooth and no longer gritty. Beat in the parmesan until glossy and loose enough to spoon.
warnings: Thick polenta bloops and spits like lava as it cooks — stir with a long spoon and mind your hands.

steps.title: Assemble the bowls
steps.content: Spoon polenta into bowls and top with brisket and gravy.
steps.detail: Spoon the soft parmesan polenta into warm bowls and make a little well in each. Pile the shredded brisket and its gravy on top, then finish with a scatter of extra parmesan and a good crack of pepper.

title: Potato Green Bean Dijon Salad
description: Warm baby potatoes and just-crisp green beans in a sharp dijon-lemon dressing, with jammy boiled eggs and plenty of parsley.
storage: Best warm or same-day. Keeps 1 day in the fridge; the beans lose a little colour but the flavour holds — bring back to room temperature before serving.
make_ahead: Boil the potatoes, beans and eggs and whisk the dressing ahead, then toss it all together just before serving so nothing goes soggy.

substitutions.swap: wholegrain mustard for a seedier dressing
a splash of red wine vinegar
a few sliced spring onions for a milder bite

steps.title: Boil the potatoes
steps.content: Simmer even-sized potato chunks in salted water until tender.
steps.detail: Cut the potatoes into even bite-size chunks so they cook at the same rate, and put them in the pot covered with cold, well-salted water. Bring to the boil and simmer for 12–15 min until a knife slides in easily but they still hold their shape. Lift them out with a slotted spoon, keeping the water on the boil.
tips: Starting potatoes in cold water lets the outside and centre cook evenly, so you don't get mushy edges.

steps.title: Boil the eggs
steps.content: Simmer eggs for jammy yolks, then cool and peel.
steps.detail: Lower the eggs into the same simmering water and cook for 7–8 min for set whites and just-jammy yolks. Scoop them into a bowl of cold water to stop the cooking, then peel under the tap. Cut into halves or quarters when you're ready to plate.

steps.title: Blanch the green beans
steps.content: Cook beans briefly until bright and crisp-tender, then refresh.
steps.detail: Trim the green beans and drop them into the boiling water for 2–3 min until they turn vivid green and are crisp-tender with a little bite. Scoop straight into cold water to lock in the colour, then drain well so they don't water down the dressing.

steps.title: Whisk the dijon dressing
steps.content: Whisk dijon, lemon and oil, and steep the red onion in it.
steps.detail: In a jar or bowl, whisk the dijon with the juice of the lemon, the olive oil, salt and pepper until it thickens into a smooth, punchy dressing. Slice the red onion wafer-thin and stir it into the dressing to steep — the acid softens its sharp bite while everything else cools.

steps.title: Toss and serve
steps.content: Dress the warm potatoes and beans, top with eggs and parsley.
steps.detail: While the potatoes are still warm, tip them into a bowl with the beans and pour over the dressing and steeped onion — warm potatoes drink up the flavour. Chop the parsley and toss most of it through. Pile onto a platter, nestle in the halved eggs, and finish with the last of the parsley and a grind of pepper.

title: Tofu Peanut Lettuce Cups
description: Crisp golden tofu tossed in a lime-peanut sauce, piled into cool lettuce cups with ribbons of carrot and fresh coriander.
storage: The tofu is best hot and crisp. Keep the parts separate in the fridge for a day and build fresh, as the tofu softens once it's dressed.
leftovers: Stuff any leftovers into a wrap with extra salad for an easy lunch.

substitutions.swap: tempeh, or chicken if it needn't be vegan
any smooth nut butter, such as cashew or almond
baby cos leaves or crisp iceberg cups

steps.title: Press and cube the tofu
steps.content: Drain and press the tofu, then cut into cubes.
steps.detail: Drain the tofu and wrap it in a clean tea towel, then sit something heavy like a chopping board on top for 10 min to squeeze out water. Unwrap and cut it into 2 cm cubes. The drier the tofu, the better it crisps in the pan.
tips: Pressing out the water is the difference between golden, crisp tofu and a soft, steamy block.

steps.title: Mix the peanut sauce
steps.content: Whisk peanut butter, lime, soy and garlic into a pourable sauce.
steps.detail: In a small bowl, whisk the peanut butter with the juice of the lime, the soy sauce and the finely grated garlic. Loosen it with a tablespoon or two of warm water until it's smooth and just pourable — it should coat a spoon but still drizzle. Taste and balance with a little more lime or soy.

steps.title: Prep cups and carrot
steps.content: Separate the lettuce cups; peel carrot into ribbons.
steps.detail: Gently pull the lettuce apart into whole cup-shaped leaves, then wash and pat them dry. Peel the carrots into long ribbons with a vegetable peeler, or coarsely grate them. Pick over the coriander so you've got a pile of fresh leaves ready to go.

steps.title: Crisp the tofu
steps.content: Fry the tofu in hot oil until golden on most sides.
steps.detail: Heat the oil in the non-stick pan over medium-high. Add the tofu in a single layer and leave it undisturbed until the underside is golden before turning, cooking for about 8–10 min total until crisp and browned on most sides. Resist stirring too soon or it won't colour.
warnings: Any water clinging to the tofu will make the hot oil spit, so pat the cubes dry and lower them in gently.

steps.title: Glaze and pile up
steps.content: Toss tofu in the sauce and spoon into the lettuce cups.
steps.detail: Take the pan off the heat and toss the hot tofu through most of the peanut sauce so each cube is glazed. Spoon it into the lettuce cups, top with carrot ribbons and coriander, and drizzle over the last of the sauce. Serve with lime wedges for squeezing.

title: Sweet Potato Haloumi Fritters
description: Golden fritters of grated sweet potato and salty haloumi, crisp at the edges and soft in the middle, with a cool minty lemon yoghurt.
storage: Fritters keep 2 days in the fridge. Re-crisp them in a hot dry pan or the oven rather than the microwave, which turns them soft.
make_ahead: Grate and mix the batter a few hours ahead; give it another squeeze to shed any liquid just before frying.
leftovers: Tuck cold fritters into a wrap with salad and a smear of the minty yoghurt.

substitutions.swap: feta for a softer, saltier tang — crumble it through
grated potato or pumpkin
sour cream, or thick coconut yoghurt to keep it dairy-light

steps.title: Grate and drain sweet potato
steps.content: Coarsely grate the sweet potato, salt it, and squeeze out the liquid.
steps.detail: Peel and coarsely grate the sweet potato into a bowl. Toss it with a small pinch of salt and leave it for 5 min to draw out moisture, then pile it into a clean tea towel and wring hard over the sink. Getting it dry is the secret to fritters that crisp rather than steam.

steps.title: Grate haloumi, mix the batter
steps.content: Coarsely grate haloumi and mix with the sweet potato, egg and flour.
steps.detail: Coarsely grate the haloumi and slice the spring onions. Add them to the squeezed sweet potato with the eggs, flour and a grind of pepper, and mix into a thick batter that just holds together when you press a spoonful. Add a touch more flour if it feels loose.
tips: Haloumi is very salty, so there's no need for extra salt in the batter itself.

steps.title: Make the minty yoghurt
steps.content: Stir yoghurt with mint, lemon and a pinch of salt.
steps.detail: Finely chop the mint and stir it through the yoghurt with the zest and a squeeze of the lemon and a small pinch of salt. Taste and add more lemon if you like it sharper. Keep it in the fridge so it's cold against the hot fritters.

steps.title: Fry the fritters
steps.content: Fry heaped spoonfuls in oil until deep golden on both sides.
steps.detail: Heat a film of oil in the pan over medium heat. Drop in heaped spoonfuls of batter and flatten each into a patty with the back of the spoon. Fry for 3–4 min a side until deep golden and cooked through the middle, pressing gently to check they're set. Work in batches and top up the oil as needed.
warnings: Keep the heat at medium — too hot and the outside burns before the centre cooks. Lay fritters away from you to avoid oil splashes.

steps.title: Stack and serve
steps.content: Stack the fritters and top with the minty yoghurt.
steps.detail: Drain the fritters briefly on paper towel, then stack them up on plates. Spoon over the cold minty yoghurt and scatter with the last of the spring onion and a squeeze of lemon. Best eaten warm while the edges are still crisp.

title: Sausage Roll Tray
description: A tray of proper Aussie sausage rolls — herby pork mince rolled in golden puff pastry, glazed and baked until crisp and flaky.
storage: Keep 3 days in an airtight container in the fridge and reheat in a hot oven to crisp the pastry. Baked rolls freeze for 2 months.
make_ahead: Assemble and chill (or freeze) the rolls uncooked, then egg-wash and bake straight from cold — add a few minutes if frozen.
leftovers: Cold sausage rolls are lunchbox gold with a squeeze of tomato sauce.

substitutions.swap: beef mince, or half pork and half beef
grated day-old bread, or rolled oats
shortcrust for a sturdier, less flaky roll

steps.title: Soften the onion
steps.content: Cook grated onion and garlic in oil until sweet, then cool.
steps.detail: Finely grate or very finely dice the onion and cook it gently in the oil with the minced garlic for about 5 min until soft and sweet-smelling. Raw onion never softens in the short bake, so this step keeps the filling from being crunchy. Spread it out to cool — you don't want it hot when it hits the pork.
warnings: Garlic burns and turns bitter quickly, so keep the heat gentle and stir it often.

steps.title: Mix the filling
steps.content: Combine pork, breadcrumbs, egg, tomato paste, parsley and the cooled onion.
steps.detail: Put the pork mince in a bowl with the breadcrumbs, one egg, the tomato paste, chopped parsley, the cooled onion and garlic, and a good pinch of salt and pepper. Mix with your hands just until combined — over-working makes it dense. The breadcrumbs and egg bind it and keep it juicy.
warnings: Wash your hands and the bowl well after handling raw pork, and keep it away from the pastry until it's enclosed.

steps.title: Roll the logs
steps.content: Spoon filling along each pastry half and roll to enclose.
steps.detail: Cut each pastry sheet in half. Spoon a log of filling along one long edge of each piece. Brush the far edge with a little beaten egg, then roll the pastry over the filling to enclose it, finishing with the seam underneath so it stays sealed.

steps.title: Cut, glaze and vent
steps.content: Cut into short rolls, brush with egg and snip a vent.
steps.detail: Heat the oven to 200°C fan and line the tray. Cut each log into short rolls and space them out on the tray, seam-down. Brush the tops all over with beaten egg for a glossy finish, and snip a small vent in each so steam can escape as they bake.

steps.title: Bake until golden
steps.content: Bake until puffed, deep golden and cooked through.
steps.detail: Bake for 25–30 min until the pastry is puffed and a deep, even golden brown and the filling is cooked right through with no pink. If they're browning too fast, drop the heat a touch for the last few minutes. Rest for a couple of minutes before serving with tomato sauce.
warnings: Keep the oven door shut for the first 20 min — an early peek lets the heat out and the puff pastry can sink.
Make sure the pork is cooked all the way through, with no pink in the centre.

title: Banana Cinnamon Pancakes
description: Fluffy pancakes with sweet mashed banana and warm cinnamon folded through the batter, stacked with maple syrup and fresh banana coins.
storage: Best fresh off the pan. Leftovers keep 2 days in the fridge or freeze between sheets of baking paper; reheat in a toaster or dry pan.
make_ahead: Mix the dry ingredients the night before, then stir in the wet in the morning — don't let the finished batter sit too long or it deflates.
leftovers: Sandwich cold pancakes with peanut butter for a lunchbox treat.

substitutions.swap: any milk — oat or almond both work well
self-raising flour; if you use it, leave out the baking powder
honey, or a dusting of icing sugar

steps.title: Mash the banana
steps.content: Mash one banana smooth; slice the other for serving.
steps.detail: Mash one banana in a bowl with a fork until mostly smooth with just a few lumps — the riper and spottier it is, the sweeter your pancakes. Slice the second banana into coins and set aside to pile on top at the end.
tips: A really ripe, freckled banana brings enough natural sweetness that you'll barely need the syrup.

steps.title: Whisk and rest the batter
steps.content: Combine dry and wet, stir until just mixed, then rest.
steps.detail: Whisk the flour, baking powder and cinnamon in a bowl. Make a well and add the egg, milk and mashed banana, then stir just until combined — a few lumps are fine, and over-mixing makes tough, flat pancakes. Let the batter rest for 5 min to thicken and start bubbling.

steps.title: Heat the pan
steps.content: Melt a little butter in the pan over medium-low heat.
steps.detail: Set the non-stick pan over medium-low and melt a small knob of butter, swirling to coat the base. The butter should foam gently but not brown — if it browns instantly the pan is too hot and the pancakes will scorch before the middle cooks.

steps.title: Cook the pancakes
steps.content: Ladle rounds and cook until bubbles form, then flip.
steps.detail: Ladle rounds of batter into the pan, leaving space between them. Cook for 2–3 min until bubbles rise and pop across the surface and the edges look set, then flip and cook another 1–2 min until golden and springy to the touch. Wipe the pan and add a little more butter between batches.

steps.title: Stack and drizzle
steps.content: Stack the pancakes with banana coins and maple syrup.
steps.detail: Stack the warm pancakes on plates and scatter over the banana coins. Drizzle generously with maple syrup and finish with a light dusting of cinnamon. Serve straight away while they're warm and fluffy.
